title: Accommodation import short-link resolution + adapter enrichment

tasks.title: Confirm worktree dev env + import rate limit
tasks.description:
Verify that the SPEC-257 worktree dev environment is correctly set up before writing any code. Specifically:

1. Confirm `apps/api/.env.local` has the import rate limit raised to 100/h (the same raise applied during the SPEC-222 Chrome smoke). Look for a key like `HOSPEDA_IMPORT_RATE_LIMIT_PER_HOUR` or similar — grep `apps/api/.env.local` and `apps/api/src/utils/env.ts` to find the actual key name.
2. Confirm the web app is running (or can be started) on port 4422 (`apps/web`), the API on 3102 (`apps/api`), and the worktree DB on 5436 (PostgreSQL). These ports were established in the SPEC-222 smoke (see memory `spec-222-smoke.md`).
3. If any env key is missing or the port config is wrong, document the delta — but do NOT start coding. Fix the config only; no logic changes.

This task has no code output. Acceptance: every env/port check passes or the gap is documented in a comment in this task.

Key files to inspect: `apps/api/.env.local`, `apps/web/.env.local`, `apps/api/src/utils/env.ts`.

subtasks.title: Confirm import rate limit key in apps/api/.env.local is raised to 100/h
Confirm web port 4422, API port 3102, DB port 5436 in env files

tasks.title: Add resolveOnly mode to safeExternalFetch
tasks.description:
Add a `resolveOnly` flag to `safeExternalFetch` in `packages/utils/src/safe-fetch.ts` so callers can discover where a redirect chain lands without reading the terminal page body.

**What to change:**

1. Add `readonly resolveOnly?: boolean;` to the `SafeFetchInput` interface (default `false`). JSDoc: "Follow redirects and return the terminal URL without reading its body. `maxBytes` is not applied to the terminal response."
2. In `_doFetch`, the existing `while (true)` redirect loop classifies responses as 3xx (redirect) or terminal (non-redirect). In the **terminal branch only**, when `resolveOnly` is `true`:
   - Skip `readBodyCapped` entirely.
   - Cancel/dump the body stream (so the underlying connection is released).
   - Return `{ ok: true, status, body: '', finalUrl: currentUrl }`.
3. **Critical: preserve the #1747 undici-7 agent-destroy fix.** The agent (undici `Agent` instance) MUST be destroyed AFTER the terminal result is returned, inside the same `try/finally` block that already handles agent cleanup. Do NOT move the `agent.destroy()` call before the return — that was the exact bug in prod (SSRF-safe fetch broke because agent was destroyed before the response was consumed). Review the existing agent-destroy ordering in the function before writing any code.
4. The redirect-following branch (3xx responses) is unchanged — only the terminal branch gains the resolveOnly shortcut.
5. Default mode (`resolveOnly` absent or `false`) is byte-for-byte unchanged: body is streamed and capped, `finalUrl` and `body` returned as before.

**Key file:** `packages/utils/src/safe-fetch.ts`

AC-2.1: resolveOnly returns terminal URL without body.
AC-2.2: resolveOnly on a non-redirect returns ok=true, finalUrl=input, body=''.
AC-2.3: maxBytes is NOT applied to the terminal response under resolveOnly.
AC-2.4: redirect cap still fires under resolveOnly (SafeFetchBlocked).
AC-2.5: default mode unchanged.

subtasks.title: Add resolveOnly to SafeFetchInput interface with JSDoc
Implement terminal no-body branch in _doFetch when resolveOnly=true
Verify agent-destroy ordering is preserved (not regressed from #1747)
Confirm default mode is unchanged (no body change, no finalUrl change)

tasks.title: Rewire resolveCanonicalUrl to resolveOnly
tasks.description:
Rewire the `resolveCanonicalUrl()` function in `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.ts` to use the new `resolveOnly` mode from T-002.

**Current behavior (broken):** `resolveCanonicalUrl` calls `safeExternalFetch({ url, timeoutMs, maxBytes: 512 })`. The 512-byte cap fires on the terminal Google Maps canonical page (large HTML), returning `SafeFetchBlocked` with a lost `finalUrl`. This causes the import to fall back to the original short link, which the Google adapter cannot parse — resulting in an empty draft.

**New behavior:** Call `safeExternalFetch({ url: inputUrl, timeoutMs, maxRedirects: 5, resolveOnly: true })`. Drop `maxBytes: 512` entirely. Keep the existing fallback semantics:
- If the call returns `ok === true && finalUrl !== inputUrl`: return `finalUrl` (the resolved canonical URL).
- Otherwise (error, no redirect, SSRF blocked, same URL): return `inputUrl` unchanged.

The rest of the orchestration (Step 1b → `detectSource` → `_pickAdapter` → `extract` on the effective URL) is untouched. The resolved URL flows to `detectSource` which routes `www.google.com/maps/place/...` to `GooglePlacesAdapter`.

**Key file:** `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.ts`

AC-1.1: maps.app.goo.gl resolves to canonical Google Maps URL without fetching terminal body.
AC-1.2: resolved URL flows to GooglePlacesAdapter and prefills name + coords.
AC-1.3: on failure, degrades gracefully — never 5xx.

subtasks.title: Replace safeExternalFetch({ maxBytes:512 }) with safeExternalFetch({ resolveOnly:true, maxRedirects:5 })
Confirm fallback semantics (return inputUrl on failure/no-redirect) are preserved

tasks.title: Enrich Airbnb adapter output mapping
tasks.description:
Extend the Airbnb adapter in `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/airbnb.adapter.ts` to map the `summary`, `amenities[]` and `beds` fields that the Apify Airbnb actor returns but the adapter currently ignores.

**IMPORTANT: field name caveat.** The exact field names the Apify actor returns for these three fields are NOT confirmed at task-writing time. The candidates are:
- Summary/description: `summary` or `publicDescription`
- Amenities: `amenities` (array of strings or objects) or `amenityIds`
- Bed count: `beds` (integer)

Do NOT add speculative fields to `AirbnbItem` interface before the smoke (T-010) confirms them. Instead:
1. Add the candidate optional fields to `AirbnbItem` now with a `// TODO T-010: confirm field name against real actor output` comment.
2. In `mapItemToRawExtraction`, add guarded mapping for each:
   - `summary`/`publicDescription` → `RawExtraction.summary`
   - `amenities` → `RawExtraction.amenityNames` (extract strings if objects, pass through if already strings; feeds the existing amenity resolver)
   - `beds` → `RawExtraction.extraInfo.beds`
3. After the smoke confirms the real field names (T-010), remove the speculative candidates and finalize the interface.

**Guards:** each mapping must be `if (item.fieldName !== undefined)` — absent fields must not inject `undefined` or empty values into the extraction result. Existing mapped fields (name, description, type, coords, location, images, capacity, bed/bath count, price) are NOT to be touched.

**Key file:** `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/airbnb.adapter.ts`

AC-4.1: summary/publicDescription → RawExtraction.summary.
AC-4.2: amenities array → RawExtraction.amenityNames (existing resolver).
AC-4.3: beds → RawExtraction.extraInfo.beds.
AC-4.4: absent fields are omitted cleanly; existing fields unaffected.

subtasks.title: Add candidate optional fields (summary/publicDescription, amenities/amenityIds, beds) to AirbnbItem interface with TODO comment
Add guarded mapping: summary -> RawExtraction.summary
Add guarded mapping: amenities -> RawExtraction.amenityNames
Add guarded mapping: beds -> RawExtraction.extraInfo.beds

tasks.title: Update Booking + Google import help copy (i18n)
tasks.description:
Update the import help panel copy in the three locale files to guide hosts on which URLs to use for Booking and Google.

**Files to change:**
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/es/host.json`
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/en/host.json`
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/host.json`

**What to update:**

1. **Booking entry** — `host.importFromUrl.help.platforms.booking.steps`: rewrite the steps to instruct the host to:
   - Open the Booking.com property page in a browser.
   - Copy the URL from the address bar (it should look like `booking.com/hotel/...`).
   - Explicitly warn that a `Share-...` link (the kind shared via the Booking mobile app) **will not work** because of a Booking security check.
   - Keep the `.example` value as a canonical `booking.com/hotel/...` URL.

2. **Google entry** (optional, if the key exists) — `host.importFromUrl.help.platforms.google.steps`: optionally add a note that mobile share links from Google Maps (`maps.app.goo.gl/...`) now work correctly in addition to the full canonical URL.

**No component change needed.** `apps/web/src/components/accommodation-import/ImportFromUrl.client.tsx` (or equivalent) already renders these i18n keys — this task is copy-only.

**Key constraint:** all three locales (es, en, pt) must have the exact same key set after editing — no new keys in one locale without the equivalent in the others.

AC-3.1: Booking entry warns against Share-... links and instructs canonical URL.
AC-3.2: copy exists in es, en, pt with no missing-key warnings.

subtasks.title: Update booking.steps in es/host.json with canonical URL instruction + Share-... warning
Update booking.steps in en/host.json (same content, translated)
Update booking.steps in pt/host.json (same content, translated)

tasks.title: Non-mocked safe-fetch resolve-only integration test
tasks.description:
Add a NON-mocked integration test for the `resolveOnly` mode in `packages/utils/test/safe-fetch.integration.test.ts`. This is the undici-7 regression guard: a mocked test would not catch the class of bugs where undici's agent-destroy lifecycle breaks body streaming (the exact failure mode of the prod bug fixed in PR #1747).

**Test scenario to add:**

Using the existing real-HTTPS-server harness in the test file (which already spins up a local HTTPS server with a self-signed openssl cert for integration testing):

1. Add a route chain: `GET /short → 302 redirect → /canonical` where `/canonical` returns a 200 response with a **body larger than any reasonable `maxBytes` value** (e.g., 10 KB of filler text).
2. Call `safeExternalFetch({ url: 'https://localhost:<port>/short', resolveOnly: true })`.
3. Assert:
   - `result.ok === true`
   - `result.finalUrl` ends with `/canonical` (the terminal URL was resolved)
   - `result.body === ''` (the large terminal body was NOT read)

**Why non-mocked matters:** if resolveOnly were tested only with mocks, a regression in the undici-7 agent-destroy ordering (like the prod bug) would pass tests but break in production, exactly as happened before PR #1747.

**Also add (optional but recommended):** a separate test verifying that the non-resolveOnly default mode on the same route chain still returns the full body (regression guard for AC-2.5).

**Key file:** `packages/utils/test/safe-fetch.integration.test.ts`

Related: AC-2.1, AC-2.3, AC-2.5.

subtasks.title: Add /short→302→/canonical route with large body to existing HTTPS test server setup
Assert resolveOnly=true: ok=true, finalUrl ends with /canonical, body===''
Assert that terminal body larger than maxBytes does NOT produce SafeFetchBlocked under resolveOnly

tasks.title: Unit tests: resolveCanonicalUrl rewire + adapter routing
tasks.description:
Add unit tests for the changes made in T-003 to `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.ts`.

**Tests to write (in the existing service test file, typically `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/accommodation-import.service.test.ts` or similar):**

1. **resolveCanonicalUrl — happy path:** mock `safeExternalFetch` to return `{ ok: true, finalUrl: 'https://www.google.com/maps/place/Cheroga/@-32.48,-58.23/...', body: '' }` for input `https://maps.app.goo.gl/dCaudGsZ8r9fKvWk9`. Assert `resolveCanonicalUrl` returns the resolved canonical URL.
2. **resolveCanonicalUrl — no redirect (same URL):** mock `safeExternalFetch` to return `{ ok: true, finalUrl: inputUrl, body: '' }`. Assert the function returns `inputUrl` (fallback semantics).
3. **resolveCanonicalUrl — failure:** mock `safeExternalFetch` to return `{ ok: false }` or throw. Assert the function returns `inputUrl` (graceful degradation, no throw).
4. **Orchestration routing — resolved Google URL routes to GooglePlacesAdapter:** mock `resolveCanonicalUrl` to return a `www.google.com/maps/place/...` URL. Confirm the orchestrator's `detectSource` + `_pickAdapter` maps this to `GooglePlacesAdapter`, not the raw Google Maps Embed path or an unknown adapter.

**Key file:** `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/` (find the existing test file — do NOT create a parallel test file if one already exists).

AC-1.1, AC-1.3.

subtasks.title: Test resolveCanonicalUrl returns resolved URL when safeExternalFetch succeeds with different finalUrl
Test resolveCanonicalUrl returns inputUrl when finalUrl === inputUrl
Test resolveCanonicalUrl returns inputUrl on safeExternalFetch failure (no throw)
Test orchestration routes resolved www.google.com/maps URL to GooglePlacesAdapter

tasks.title: Unit tests: Airbnb mapping enrichment
tasks.description:
Add unit tests for the Airbnb adapter enrichment changes made in T-004, covering `mapItemToRawExtraction` in `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation-import/adapters/airbnb.adapter.ts`.

**Tests to write (in the existing adapter test file, likely `packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation-import/adapters/airbnb.adapter.test.ts`):**

1. **summary present:** given an `AirbnbItem` with `summary: 'A beautiful cabin'`, assert `mapItemToRawExtraction` returns `{ ..., summary: 'A beautiful cabin' }`.
2. **summary absent:** given an `AirbnbItem` without `summary` (undefined), assert the returned object does NOT have a `summary` key (or it's explicitly `undefined` — but NOT an empty string or null that could pollute the draft).
3. **amenities present (string array):** given `amenities: ['WiFi', 'Pool']`, assert `amenityNames` is `['WiFi', 'Pool']`.
4. **amenities present (object array):** if the actor returns `amenities: [{ name: 'WiFi' }, { name: 'Pool' }]`, assert the adapter extracts `['WiFi', 'Pool']` (confirm the shape from T-010 first; skip this variant if the actor always returns strings).
5. **amenities absent:** assert the field is omitted cleanly.
6. **beds present:** given `beds: 3`, assert `extraInfo.beds === 3`.
7. **beds absent:** assert `extraInfo.beds` is not set.
8. **Existing fields unchanged:** use a fixture with all new fields AND all existing fields; assert name/description/type/coords/price/etc. are identical to the pre-enrichment snapshot.

**Key file:** find the existing test file path with `find packages/service-core/test -name 'airbnb*'`.

AC-4.1, AC-4.2, AC-4.3, AC-4.4.

subtasks.title: Test summary field: present maps to RawExtraction.summary; absent omits field
Test amenities field: present maps to RawExtraction.amenityNames array; absent omits field
Test beds field: present maps to RawExtraction.extraInfo.beds; absent omits field
Test existing fields unchanged (regression snapshot)

tasks.title: i18n suite passes (no missing keys)
tasks.description:
Run the i18n vitest suite to confirm that after the host.json edits from T-005, all three locales (es, en, pt) are in parity and there are no missing-key warnings.

**Command:** `pnpm exec vitest run packages/i18n` (NOT `pnpm test` — the full suite causes OOM; run one file at a time).

**What this validates:**
- The new/updated Booking steps keys exist in all three locales.
- No locale has an extra key that the others lack.
- The i18n test suite passes 100% (currently at 638 tests per the SPEC-195 memory entry — expect that count to stay equal or increase).

**If the suite fails:** fix the key parity issue in `packages/i18n/src/locales/` before marking this task done. A common failure is adding a new sub-key in one locale and forgetting to mirror it in the others.

**Key files:** `packages/i18n/src/locales/es/host.json`, `packages/i18n/src/locales/en/host.json`, `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/host.json`.

AC-3.2.

subtasks.title: Run pnpm exec vitest run packages/i18n and confirm all tests pass

tasks.title: Real Chrome smoke + confirm Airbnb actor fields
tasks.description:
Run a real Chrome smoke against the local worktree environment (web :4422, API :3102, DB :5436) to validate the end-to-end flows and confirm the actual Airbnb Apify actor field names for the T-004 enrichment.

**Environment prerequisites:** servers running (`pnpm cli wt:up` or start manually), rate limit at 100/h (T-001).

**Smoke scenarios:**

1. **Google short link:** paste `https://maps.app.goo.gl/dCaudGsZ8r9fKvWk9` ("Cheroga") into `/es/publicar/nueva/`. Assert:
   - The import resolves and the draft prefills with the place name and coordinates.
   - The "Importado · NN% · ..." badge appears.
   - No console errors about resolveCanonicalUrl.

2. **Airbnb enrichment + field confirmation:** paste `https://www.airbnb.com.ar/rooms/817515602448448452` into the import. Inspect the prefilled draft:
   - Open the network tab and check the `/api/v1/protected/accommodations/import` response body.
   - Find the actual field names the Apify actor returns for description, amenities and beds in the raw response payload.
   - Compare to T-004's `AirbnbItem` interface; if the candidate field names differ, update the interface in T-004 now (finalize the TODO T-010 comments).
   - Assert that summary/amenityNames/beds are present in the prefilled draft if the actor returned them.

3. **Booking help copy:** navigate to the import form, expand the help panel for Booking. Assert the updated copy renders (mentions `booking.com/hotel/...` and warns against Share-... links). No missing-key `[MISSING: ...]` tokens visible.

**Tests must run one file at a time** (`pnpm exec vitest run <path>`) to avoid OOM.

AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-4.1, AC-4.2, AC-4.3, AC-3.1.

tasks.title: Cross-ref SPEC-222 + open PR to staging
tasks.description:
Finalize the SPEC-257 implementation, add a cross-reference to SPEC-222, and open the PR to staging.

**Steps:**

1. **Cross-reference SPEC-222:** Add a note to `apps/web/src/components/accommodation-import/` or the relevant CLAUDE.md / docs file stating that SPEC-257 closed the short-link resolution follow-up from SPEC-222 (PRs #1747 and #1748). This helps future developers understand the connection without re-reading the spec history.

2. **Commit discipline:**
   - Stage files individually (never `git add .` or `git add -A`).
   - Each commit covers one logical change: safe-fetch change, service rewire, adapter enrichment, i18n copy, tests.
   - Commit messages follow Conventional Commits: `fix(utils): add resolveOnly mode to safeExternalFetch (SPEC-257)`, `fix(service-core): rewire resolveCanonicalUrl to resolveOnly (SPEC-257)`, etc.
   - Do NOT add AI attribution (no Co-Authored-By, no "Generated with Claude Code").
   - Lowercase subject line.

3. **Final checks before PR:**
   - `pnpm exec vitest run packages/utils/test/safe-fetch.integration.test.ts` — green.
   - `pnpm exec vitest run packages/service-core/test/...` — green (relevant test files only, one at a time).
   - `pnpm exec vitest run packages/i18n` — green.
   - `pnpm lint` (or `pnpm check`) — green.
   - `pnpm typecheck` — green.

4. **Open PR to `staging`:** use `gh pr create --base staging --head spec/SPEC-257-import-short-link-resolution`. Title: `fix(import): short-link resolution + Airbnb enrichment (SPEC-257)`. Body: summarize what changed and reference SPEC-222 PRs #1747/#1748.

5. **Owner merges after CI green** — do not force-merge with `--admin` unless there is a known pre-existing baseline failure unrelated to SPEC-257.

AC-1.1, AC-1.2, AC-3.1, AC-3.2, AC-4.1, AC-4.2, AC-4.3.

subtasks.title: Stage and commit changes atomically (one commit per logical change)
Run lint + typecheck + tests (one file at a time) — all green
Open PR to staging with gh pr create --base staging
